name: Cory Booker
subject: No human is illegal -- no matter what Donald Trump says.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cory 2020
body:
| | |
---
| Team, Inscribed on the Statue of Liberty are the words "give me your tired, your poor, your huddled masses yearning to breathe free." As a nation of immigrants, we cannot let that sentiment become less true under a Trump Administration. It is horrifying that President Trump just stood in the Rose Garden of the White House with the intention of terrorizing immigrant communities, our friends and neighbors. But make no mistake: **he lost.** Every person will be counted by the Census. **Hate cannot and will not win.** [**There is strength in numbers and in our collective voice. Join me in showing President Trump that Americans will not tolerate our immigrant friends and neighbors being targeted.**](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0879/5fca0d19/b1b1b22/2918242f/2032604810/VEsH/ "https://act.corybooker.com/page/s/Census?source=em_pet_20190711") President Trump has repeatedly violated some of our core national values in pursuit of his aggressive, divisive, inhumane, and un-American immigration policies. Instead of uniting our nation, President Trump wants to pit Americans against one another by provoking fear and anger. **We are better than that, America.** [**Join me in denouncing President Trump 's actions and demand better.**](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0879/5fca0d19/b1b1b22/2918242f/2032604810/VEsE/ "https://act.corybooker.com/page/s/Census?source=em_pet_20190711") Fighting with you for a better tomorrow,
Cory

This campaign is moving quickly. If you want the most up-to-date information, follow Cory on [Instagram](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0879/5fca0d19/b1b1b22/2918242d/2032604810/VEsC/ "https://www.instagram.com/corybooker/"), [Twitter](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0879/5fca0d19/b1b1b22/2918242c/2032604810/VEsD/ "https://twitter.com/corybooker"), and [Facebook](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0879/5fca0d19/b1b1b22/2918242b/2032604810/VEsA/ "https://www.facebook.com/corybooker").
It's time for leadership that can reignite our spirit of common purpose. That takes all of us. We believe these emails are an important way to stay in touch, but if at any point you'd like to stop getting these updates, you can unsubscribe [here](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0879/5fca0d19/b1b1b22/2918242a/2032604810/VEsB/ "https://act.corybooker.com/unsubscribe").

body:
| | |
---
| Team -- In the last week, we learned that both Elizabeth Warren and Julian Castro have reached the 130,000 unique donors needed to qualify for the fall debates. I think that's great -- their voices belong on the national stage. Now, seven candidates -- Bernie Sanders, Joe Biden, Kamala Harris, Andrew Yang, Pete Buttigieg, Elizabeth Warren and Julian Castro -- have crossed the donor threshold. And we know a few more are almost there. After the first DNC debate, over 30,000 people stepped up and became first-time donors to our campaign for president. We may be an underdog but one thing remains clear: When our message is heard, people are excited to join this grassroots movement. [**If you think my voice should be part of the fall debates, will you chip in today to help me qualify? Even a dollar counts.**](https://act.corybooker. "http://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-20190708-em1?refcode=em_fr_20190712_CB_20190712-fr-CB-falldebates2-actives_2020ndnr_l1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=") Thank you, Cory _\---- ORIGINAL MESSAGE ----_ Team -- Five Democrats have already publicly crossed the DNC's donor threshold needed to qualify for the fall debates. Bernie Sanders did. Joe Biden did. Kamala Harris did. Andrew Yang did. And Pete Buttigieg did. Beto O'Rourke has probably reached it, too. Now the question is whether other candidates will reach that tough measure of grassroots strength before the cutoff. **And I 'll be honest: We're just about 15,000 donors shy of the 130,000 benchmark.** [_**So I need to ask directly: If you want to see me in those debates, will you make your first donation today and get me one step closer to qualifying?**_](https://act.corybooker. "http://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-20190708-em1?refcode=em_fr_20190712_CB_20190712-fr-CB-falldebates2-actives_2020ndnr_l2&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=") Thanks to people like you, we've taken a big leap toward this goal. In the 12 days since the first debate, over 20,000 people have decided to become a donor to our campaign. I'm so encouraged to know that thousands of Americans heard what I had to say that night and decided I should be part of future debates, too. But we have thousands more to go -- and not too many weeks left to hit that DNC target. [_**Chip in if you can today and help send me to the fall debates.**_](https://act.corybooker. name: Teresa Tomlinson
subject: ANNOUNCEMENT: Why I won’t accept corporate PAC money on my campaign
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TERESA TOMLINSON FOR SENATE
body:
[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP07/1/74264/images/tt2020%20email%20header.png]
Friend,
I'd like to share some very exciting news with you, today I am announcing my campaign has not, and will not, accept a single penny from corporate PACs. Period.

The role of money in politics has long held us back from fully realizing our democratic ideals, and after the Supreme Court's decision in Citizens United v. FEC , dark money has exerted an even greater influence on our elections — and the legislation that gets passed between them.

That is why I am rejecting all corporate PAC money on our campaign for U.S. Senate.
Your voices are simply too important to be drowned out by the corporations and special interests whose massive donations act as a quid pro quo when politicians are elected to office.

We can win this election without taking corporate PAC money, but only with your help. Can you sign our petition right now and join us in this pledge? We're counting on you. [https://teresatomlinson.com/take-the-no-corporate-pac-money-pledge/?emci=5e0de7bb-bfa4-e911-bcd0-281878391efb&emdi=0108911c-d3a4-e911-bcd0-281878391efb&ceid=463701&contactdata=jg6jDVUNby5jtV9VwRhSvWFm9D50Bd3CH9c2Chm%2B8v9nRQOgqDPqlMeXclyPcD9bTTegkhT2vUrxHRHCbrbtbKPTLRvnl%2BlXR5oyT4MWYlY%3D&refcode=em071219&refcode2=0108911c-d3a4-e911-bcd0-281878391efb]
SIGN HERE [https://teresatomlinson.com/take-the-no-corporate-pac-money-pledge/?emci=5e0de7bb-bfa4-e911-bcd0-281878391efb&emdi=0108911c-d3a4-e911-bcd0-281878391efb&ceid=463701&contactdata=jg6jDVUNby5jtV9VwRhSvWFm9D50Bd3CH9c2Chm%2B8v9nRQOgqDPqlMeXclyPcD9bTTegkhT2vUrxHRHCbrbtbKPTLRvnl%2BlXR5oyT4MWYlY%3D&refcode=em071219&refcode2=0108911c-d3a4-e911-bcd0-281878391efb]
Thank you for standing up for free and fair elections as we build this movement to transform Georgia and our country.
Yours truly,
Teresa

Friend,

**Governor Holcomb’s first fundraising period of 2019 was a success!** We saw an historic outpouring of support of support from Hoosiers, and Governor Holcomb is so grateful.

**Because of support from Hoosiers like you this fundraising period, Governor Holcomb now has $6.03 million cash-on-hand,** which is more than any previous Indiana governor's cash-on-hand at the midpoint of their third year in office.

**Then, when you combine his fundraising with the Indiana Republican Party and Lt. Governor Suzanne Crouch, there’s another record -- $7.23 million in the bank! **

It's safe to say Hoosiers are seeing a trend from Governor Eric Holcomb. After posting record fundraising numbers and earning a sky-high approval rating at the end of 2018, Governor Holcomb is at it again at the end of another fundraising period. Hoosiers have placed Governor Holcomb in a historic position because of his people-focused approach to leading our state and because of the unprecedented results that approach is delivering across the state.

_Governor Holcomb relies on grassroots donations from Hoosiers like you to help keep Indiana’s momentum surging and to make this record-breaking fundraising possible. Without you, Governor Holcomb cannot continue to achieve historic accomplishments for Hoosiers._

_What are those accomplishments?_

We’ve seen record job commitments, record infrastructure investments, new workforce development opportunities and a state government that is becoming leaner and more responsive to the needs of Hoosiers under Governor Holcomb’s leadership, but we’re just getting started.

YOU are making this possible – and we want to keep this momentum going.

name: Voter Protection Project
subject: Lindsey Graham's bringing out the big guns -- this is bad
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Voter Protection Project and not authorized by any candidate or any candidate's committee
body:
---
| | Senator Lindsey Graham is bringing out the big guns to try and shut out the competition: he just announced that every major Republican in the state is endorsing him for re-election.- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - Senator Lindsey Graham is bringing out the big guns to try and shut out the competition: **he just announced that every major Republican in the state is endorsing him for re-election.** [](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d0720/72412298/43a4fcf8/610279f2/2762533777/VEsH/p/eyJlM3R6YkhWbmZYMD0iOiJ2cHBfbW9udGhseV9zcGxpdGhhcnJpc29uMiIsImUzdDJaWEp6YVc5dWZYMD0iOiJWUFBfRU1fRlJfMTkwNzEyX0dyYWhhbUJpZ0d1bnNfVTFfWDEiLCJlM3NnWVhOcklIMTkiOiIyNSJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/vpp_monthly_splitharrison2?refcode=VPP_EM_FR_190712_GrahamBigGuns_U1_X1&amount=25&recurring=auto&utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_FR_190712_GrahamBigGuns_U1_X1&source=VPP_EM_FR_190712_GrahamBigGuns_U1_X1") Graham is set to cruise through the Republican primary and can focus all his resources on taking down his Democratic opponent -- which is bad news for Americans everywhere, since Graham is one of Trump's closest allies in Congress. **Democratic challenger Jaime Harrison is our best chance to unseat Graham and take back the Senate, but he 's still got to win his primary. name: Ryan Furlong
subject: "Mitch McConnell's leadership of the United States Senate has been a big fat waste."
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tina Smith for Minnesota
body:
| [](https://action.tinaforminnesota. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tsm-email-july2019?refcode=rtw_20190712_em-full&recurring=auto&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=") Team, you need to see this new op-ed from Tina on _CNN.com_. **She holds Mitch McConnell accountable for stalling progress in the Senate and holding vote after vote on Trump 's dangerous and often unqualified nominees. **

**[I hope you can read below then chip in $5 so she can continue fighting back in the U.S. Senate against Trump's dangerous nominees to the courts. She's counting on us to have her back against the powerful interests on the other side.](https://action.tinaforminnesota. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tsm-email-july2019?refcode=rtw_20190712_em-full&recurring=auto&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=")** **[PITCH IN](https://action.tinaforminnesota. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tsm-email-july2019?refcode=rtw_20190712_em-full&recurring=auto&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=")** Taking on Trump's nominees makes Tina a target for conservative donors and wealthy special interests who want nothing more than rubber stamps for their extreme right-wing agenda. We must ensure she has the resources to counter their attacks.

Ryan Furlong
Campaign Manager _\---- Tina 's Op-Ed ----_ I came to the United States Senate 18 months ago as an accidental senator; I never expected to gain the great honor of serving in Washington. I'll never have the seniority of lions of the Senate who've been in office for decades. What I do have is a lifetime of experience as a parent and activist, small business owner, and lieutenant governor of Minnesota.

And all this experience, and basic common sense, tells me that Mitch McConnell's leadership of the United States Senate has been a big fat waste.

Americans can see this, and they are rightly frustrated with the slow pace of progress in Washington. I'm frustrated, too. I came to Washington understanding what the Senate is capable of accomplishing and knowing that I'd have myriad opportunities as a senator to get things done. And let's be clear that the problem isn't that senators on both sides of the aisle can't agree on anything. Truth is, there's plenty of work we could be doing -- if only Republican Senate Majority Leader Mitch McConnell would let us.

But McConnell has transformed the Senate into little more than the Trump administration's personnel office, the place where good ideas go to die. As of July 3, the Senate has taken 127 votes since the rules change on April 3, which drastically reduced the amount of time some nominees could be debated on the Senate floor. Just 21 of those 127 votes were related to legislation -- that's 16.5% of floor time devoted to legislative debate -- while the vast majority were devoted to pushing through the Trump administration's nominations.

He's proud of this, too. Asked what his priorities are, McConnell has said, "We are in the personnel business." What that means is that day in and day out, the work of the Senate has been reduced to voting to pack the courts with Trump-appointed, lifetime federal judges, as fast as we can. And because McConnell and the Republicans have dramatically reduced the time for debate on most of these judges -- from 30 hours to two -- they can pack the courts faster than ever before.

Voting on these nominees is part of my job as a senator, and I take this responsibility seriously. I make time to research the background of each nominee, but the truth is, there's never any real debate. McConnell puts a name on the floor, we look down at our binders, we cast our votes, and we move on to the next one. Sometimes I think about how automation is taking jobs away from American workers and wonder whether we're going to be next.

Let's take a look at a recent -- and pretty typical -- week in the Senate. McConnell put 11 nominations up for a vote, seven of them for lifetime appointments to the federal bench. One of them had never tried a case in court. One wrote a legal opinion that divorce laws apply only to heterosexual couples. Another argued that private companies should be able to offer healthcare coverage that doesn't cover basic preventative care for women, including contraceptives. And then there are the candidates who cannot even bring themselves to say that Brown v. The Board of Education, the landmark Supreme Court case from 65 years ago ending school segregation, was rightly decided.

While I try to consider each nominee on his or her merits, it's usually pretty clear why they've been selected by the Trump administration. Many are members of the right-wing Federalist Society, a shadowy organization funded by undisclosed donors and devoted to stocking the judiciary with conservative activists who will help overturn Roe v. Wade, legalize discrimination against LGBTQ+ Americans, limit the power of workers and consumers, undermine the right to vote, and generally treat the courtroom as a platform for advancing the interests of the Republican Party.

It's not just judges. McConnell is also using this new process to ram through executive appointments. I recently voted against a man who had been appointed to run the federal agency overseeing people's pensions. Why? Because he has no experience with pensions whatsoever, apart from hoping to get one someday. Did I mention he's Mitch McConnell's brother-in-law? Didn't matter. The guy got voted through, and we were on to the next one.

Every day, I talk to Democratic and Republican colleagues with lots of ideas about the work we should be doing. Passing the Violence Against Women Act, protecting our elections from cyberattacks from hostile nations, stabilizing our health care system, expanding rural broadband -- these are all issues where most Democrats and Republicans share an interest in getting something done. She holds Mitch McConnell accountable for stalling progress in the Senate and holding vote after vote on Trump 's dangerous and often unqualified nominees. "=" "=" Taking on Trump's nominees makes Tina a target for conservative donors and wealthy special interests who want nothing more than rubber stamps for their extreme right-wing agenda. We must ensure she has the resources to counter their attacks. Ryan Furlong Campaign Manager \---- Tina 's Op-Ed ---- I came to the United States Senate 18 months ago as an accidental senator; I never expected to gain the great honor of serving in Washington. I'll never have the seniority of lions of the Senate who've been in office for decades. What I do have is a lifetime of experience as a parent and activist, small business owner, and lieutenant governor of Minnesota. And all this experience, and basic common sense, tells me that Mitch McConnell's leadership of the United States Senate has been a big fat waste. Americans can see this, and they are rightly frustrated with the slow pace of progress in Washington. I'm frustrated, too. I came to Washington understanding what the Senate is capable of accomplishing and knowing that I'd have myriad opportunities as a senator to get things done. And let's be clear that the problem isn't that senators on both sides of the aisle can't agree on anything. Truth is, there's plenty of work we could be doing -- if only Republican Senate Majority Leader Mitch McConnell would let us. But McConnell has transformed the Senate into little more than the Trump administration's personnel office, the place where good ideas go to die. As of July 3, the Senate has taken 127 votes since the rules change on April 3, which drastically reduced the amount of time some nominees could be debated on the Senate floor. Just 21 of those 127 votes were related to legislation -- that's 16.5% of floor time devoted to legislative debate -- while the vast majority were devoted to pushing through the Trump administration's nominations. He's proud of this, too. Asked what his priorities are, McConnell has said, "We are in the personnel business." What that means is that day in and day out, the work of the Senate has been reduced to voting to pack the courts with Trump-appointed, lifetime federal judges, as fast as we can. And because McConnell and the Republicans have dramatically reduced the time for debate on most of these judges -- from 30 hours to two -- they can pack the courts faster than ever before. Voting on these nominees is part of my job as a senator, and I take this responsibility seriously. I make time to research the background of each nominee, but the truth is, there's never any real debate. McConnell puts a name on the floor, we look down at our binders, we cast our votes, and we move on to the next one. Sometimes I think about how automation is taking jobs away from American workers and wonder whether we're going to be next. Let's take a look at a recent -- and pretty typical -- week in the Senate. McConnell put 11 nominations up for a vote, seven of them for lifetime appointments to the federal bench. One of them had never tried a case in court. One wrote a legal opinion that divorce laws apply only to heterosexual couples. Another argued that private companies should be able to offer healthcare coverage that doesn't cover basic preventative care for women, including contraceptives. And then there are the candidates who cannot even bring themselves to say that Brown v. The Board of Education, the landmark Supreme Court case from 65 years ago ending school segregation, was rightly decided. While I try to consider each nominee on his or her merits, it's usually pretty clear why they've been selected by the Trump administration. Many are members of the right-wing Federalist Society, a shadowy organization funded by undisclosed donors and devoted to stocking the judiciary with conservative activists who will help overturn Roe v. Wade, legalize discrimination against LGBTQ+ Americans, limit the power of workers and consumers, undermine the right to vote, and generally treat the courtroom as a platform for advancing the interests of the Republican Party. | | |
# **President Trump on social media bias: ‘They’re playing with a lot of minds’**

President Donald J. Trump called out social media censorship and bias during a first-of-its-kind summit at the White House yesterday, warning that legislation could follow “if Silicon Valley doesn’t change its ways,” Mark Moore reports for the _New York Post_.

“I am directing my administration to explore all regulatory and legislation solutions to protect free speech and the free speech rights of all Americans — that’s you people in this room,” the President said at the Social Media Summit at the White House. “We hope to see transparency, more accountability and more freedom — that’s on both sides.”

President Trump recognized the attendees for challenging both the establishment media and corporate censors and bringing more information to light for the American people.

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=5a5c20969f&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| USMCA will provide “access to rules-based, tariff-free global trade”—something “imperative to the economic prosperity and the livelihoods of more than 634,600 workers across [Michigan],” Chuck Hadden, CEO of the Michigan Manufacturers Association, writes in [_The Detroit News_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=7746a2c94e&e=3ee4985eea). “I remain hopeful that our lawmakers will see the economic opportunities . . . The USMCA will strengthen and modernize rules to spur economic prosperity, increase access to Canada and Mexico, and finally create an even playing field for U.S. manufacturers.”
---
|
---
| “President Trump officially issued his executive order Thursday, which demands all other departments and agencies provide the Commerce Department with any information related to citizenship,” Julio Rosas and Jerry Dunleavy report in the [_Washington Examiner_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=9a7e18252e&e=3ee4985eea) _._ The President also “instructed the Commerce Department begin planning the administrative processes necessary to add the citizenship question to the census in 2030.”
---
|
---
| “The Trump administration's proposed sale of 108 M1A2T Abrams tanks to Taiwan not only serves a national security purpose — it is also a major boost to the Ohio manufacturing sector,” Russ Read writes in the [_Washington Examiner_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=7166b8800d&e=3ee4985eea). “The Abrams is produced and serviced at the Joint Systems Manufacturing Center in Lima, Ohio, a city that has seen highs and lows based on the whims of the Pentagon.”
---
|

body:
Did you see Sri's email earlier this week? The GOP will stop at nothing to strip Americans of their healthcare.

We need leaders in Washington who will stand up for our healthcare, and Sri is one of those leaders we can count on. But first, we need to flip this District and send him to Congress.

**[We can count on him to stand up for all of us. Can we count on your support to help him get there?](https://act.myngp.com/el/fg5elk_qn0hlhzgfe_FpWyZLx7mveh1Xn2nhenpRIPU=/_Qn_n8isEUpUY7mOjnNJtv4wnEc-bJ8DDRitHU9lHeY=?refcode=0712email)**

Let's flip this District together!

Thank you,

Drew Godinich, Campaign Manager
Sri for Congress 2020

* * *
**From:** Sri Preston Kulkarni
**Sent:** Tuesday, July 9, 2019
**Subject:** Hi - we must protect our healthcare

Healthcare costs are exploding, yet this Administration will stop at nothing to repeal the Affordable Care Act. Today, the 5th Circuit Court of Appeals is hearing oral arguments in the Trump Administration and 18 Republican-led states lawsuit challenging the constitutionality of the Affordable Care Act. If successful, their lawsuit would increase the cost of healthcare, eliminate protections for people with pre-existing conditions, and raise prescription drug costs.

Incredibly, Pete Olson has stood with these efforts every step of the way - endangering the healthcare of millions of Americans. Today, medical bills are the number one cause of bankruptcy in America. My father's medical treatment for cancer nearly bankrupted my family, and I've heard so many stories on the campaign trail just like mine.

It's time to fix what's not working in Washington, not play politics with the livelihood of millions of Americans. [**I 'm ready to take on the fight for our healthcare. Will you stand with me?**](https://act.myngp.com/el/fg5elk_qn0hlhzgfe_FpWyZLx7mveh1Xn2nhenpRIPU=/Zme6bvNrCDruh31lDg-k4QJaxK7hTD5DK2foLbAzjH4=)

I'm running to protect every American's access to healthcare because no family should ever have to go through what mine did. Insurance companies should not be allowed to charge people with pre-existing conditions extreme amounts for basic healthcare. Prescription drug companies shouldn't be allowed to charge whatever they want for medications that literally mean the difference between life and death.

We need leaders who tell folks what they stand for, not just what they're against. I'm standing for access to healthcare, for lowering prescription drug costs, and making sure that the millions of Americans with pre-existing conditions get the care they need and that they deserve. **[Stand with me in standing up for our healthcare.](https://act.myngp.com/el/fg5elk_qn0hlhzgfe_FpWyZLx7mveh1Xn2nhenpRIPU=/Zme6bvNrCDruh31lDg-k4QJaxK7hTD5DK2foLbAzjH4=)**

Standing strong for our healthcare,

Sri Preston Kulkarni

subject: our families deserve better

| Team, we need your help. It will only take two minutes and it costs you absolutely nothing -- but it could help make a huge impact.

We know that the period after a child is born is critical for development.

Unfortunately, the United States is failing when it comes to family and medical leave laws.

We need 500 more signatures by 6PM to demand that every member of Congress stands up for change.

[TELL CONGRESS: STRENGTHEN OUR PARENTAL LEAVE LAWS!](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cbadb9/134673f2/22a893/69968bf7/2472830415/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZGYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVkdhWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "https://www.signherenow.org/petition/paid-family-leave/bennet?source=EM_PET_B1_071219_AC_ND_RS&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=")

---

Across the world, in the vast majority of developed countries, paid leave is the standard.

In the United States? Parents are only guaranteed 12 weeks of _unpaid_ leave and that's only if the company employs over 50 people.

More than half of American businesses don't have to guarantee any leave for parents.

This is a shameful practice for the most prosperous country on Earth, and it's up to all of us to demand change.

[Let's send a message: pass real parental leave policies now!](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cbadb9/134673f2/22a893/69968bf7/2472830415/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZGYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVkdhWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "https://www.signherenow.org/petition/paid-family-leave/bennet?source=EM_PET_B1_071219_AC_ND_RS&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=")

Team Bennet

name: Rob Flaherty, Beto for America
subject: Banquet hall tilapia
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Beto for America
body:
#
Hi, it’s me again, Beto’s Digital Director.
We’ve been pretty up front about the dynamics of this race. It changes every week. Sometimes you’re up, sometimes you’re down. Sometimes you’re Hootie and sometimes you’re the Blowfish. But this week, there was some news that might shake things up: we have a self-funder joining the race, and they’re bringing $100 million along with them.
Beto...is not that kind of candidate. He’s always been about people power — about showing what we can achieve when hundreds of thousands of people come together to fund a campaign $5, $10, $20 at a time. No PACs, no lobbyists, no special interests. We’re counting on you and you alone.
[Can you make a $3 donation to our campaign today and help Beto compete against these high-dollar opponents? Our way of doing things certainly isn’t easy, but we know we can go toe-to-toe with your help.](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/18127?refcode=ema20190712-42080&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=19&akid=42080%2E444211%2EpKK_tI)
**[Contribute $3 »](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/18127?refcode=ema20190712-42080&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=20&akid=42080%2E444211%2EpKK_tI)**
That’s not how every candidate is gonna roll. I get that. But democracy shouldn’t be about which billionaire can afford the most TV ads in Cedar Rapids — no matter how well-intentioned they are. It shouldn’t be about which rich friend can start a super PAC. And it shouldn’t be about whichever candidate can invite the most lobbyists to eat banquet hall tilapia for $2,800-per-plate. It’s about talking to voters — face to face, person to person — and _earning_ their support. Not buying it.
Beto is that candidate. He shows up in places politicians don’t usually go — from a town in Iowa with a population of 300 with over 100 attendees, to an event in Nashville last week with over 1,000. He connects with people person-to-person and gives others the microphone.

He’s fighting to earn your vote the right way. And while it may put us at a _slight_ financial disadvantage if someone’s campaign can almost literally print money, I know we can make up for it with the power of you.
[Chip in any amount — it could be $1, $10, or whatever you can afford. If everyone chips in a little bit right now, we’ll have no problem competing with any self-funded billionaires who turn up.](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/18127?refcode=ema20190712-42080&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=21&akid=42080%2E444211%2EpKK_tI)
Thank you for everything.

Rob Flaherty
Digital Director, Beto for America

Paid for by Beto for America
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. This campaign is powered by grassroots supporters like you. But if you'd like to receive fewer emails, [you can do so here](https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/). If you need to remove yourself from our email list, [click here to unsubscribe](https://act.betoorourke.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=22&akid=42080%2E444211%2EpKK_tI). To contribute via check, please address to Beto for America campaign, P.O. Box 3628 El Paso, TX 79923.

body:
NAGR Previously Endorsed Kris Kobach; Now backing LaTurner | | |
---
|
---
| | Great news! Our campaign just received a major endorsement today from the National Association for Gun Rights, the same organization that supported Kris Kobach last year in his unsuccessful run for the Governor's office. Here's what they had to say:

**_“The National Association for Gun Rights PAC is proud to endorse State Treasurer Jake LaTurner for US Senate in Kansas. At a time when the Second Amendment is under assault by anti-gun politicians in both parties, it's refreshing to have a candidate like Jake LaTurner make protecting the right to bear arms a centerpiece of his campaign. Jake LaTurner not only returned his NAGR candidate survey 100% pro-gun, but he was also the leader in the fight to make Kansas the nation's fifth Constitutional Carry state."_** _-NAGR Senior Political Director Ryan Flugaur._

[Will you support our campaign by joining our Self-Defense, Concealed Carry, or 2nd Amendment Defender Levels?](https://laturnerforsenate.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=66e1e33aabb963218c502c41e&id=ed66fe4ef5&e=6882b657f8)

During my five years as a Kansas State Senator,**_I made it clear that I would not back down on gun rights for Kansans, successfully shepherding Constitutional Carry through the Kansas Senate,_** helping pass a prohibition on public funded lobbying for gun control, and blocking the restriction of concealed carry in public buildings. I also have an A+ rating from the National Rifle Association.

Our statewide and national support continues to grow as more and more people agree that _we are the conservative fighter in this race for U.S. Senate that can WIN!_ It is absolutely critical that we don't lose any seats in the Senate in 2020.

Washington D.C. needs a true conservative who will strongly defend our Second Amendment rights. I will continue to be the conservative fighter who leads on these issues, protecting the rights guaranteed by the Constitution.

[Help us show the anti-gun liberals that we mean business by contributing $45, $22, or even $9](https://laturnerforsenate.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=66e1e33aabb963218c502c41e&id=2533ec2474&e=6882b657f8)

Jake LaTurner

name: =?utf-8?Q?Indiana=20Republican=20Party?=
subject: Looking Ahead
body:
As Governor Holcomb's big announcement day comes upon us, I can't help but look ahead to Indiana's bright future...

View this email in your browser (https://mailchi.mp/indiana/looking-ahead?e=1acd9bb3d6)
https://holcombforindiana.com/events-713/?mc_cid=e6dc97c127&mc_eid=[UNIQID]

Looking Ahead

As peaceful as it is to see an empty gym, I can't wait for this place to be completely packed tomorrow for Governor Holcomb's big announcement! As Governor Holcomb's big announcement day comes upon us, I can't help but look ahead to Indiana's bright future.

Governor Holcomb’s people-focused policies are building an even brighter future for Indiana, and they will continue to do so well into the future.

Our entire state is seeing the results of these policies – more job opportunities, rising wages, more opportunities to build high-demand skills, record infrastructure investments, more Hoosiers in recovery from the opioid crisis, and all-around great government service. And he's tacking our biggest issues head-on, including working toward a systemic solution to raise teacher wages to make sure we're attracting and retaining the best educators in Indiana.

I'd love to see you tomorrow (https://holcombforindiana.com/events-713/?mc_cid=e6dc97c127&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) at the historic Hoosier Gym (they filmed "Hoosiers" there!). And I'd recommend getting there early (doors open at 11 a.m.), as we're expecting to see Hoosiers from every one of Indiana's 92 counties.

-Kyle Hupfer

P.S. If you're coming tomorrow, share this fun video on Facebook (https://www.facebook.com/HolcombForIndiana/videos/889545978088876/?__xts__[0]=68.ARCkMzdnzbDIAPvYi5TTUi4jeQrkImrCcvJzkeDGYviULRCjCagkclUe-BdnCra0N746G8EdLsSUuacec1suOcYCqu_spGmSTAW73VtD9B9gLCqOLOt5OJFv8UTLEZqPuuQX7phwQ14wAVBZdTbd6-qI6u9v-AmeRRcwL1sptcguaFkhH2ZVdEDzurlOc_03XUXk1e4r3WgHcz9TxLV8-7vIRQIQ2g3LmDZMLgMOt6LxWHBTsbolj0xhq7_KnlbcRvxb7VrzOsMSWbsVITqQw4SEx-iF8sNJ_sPvnduLZRMeBV7HXoiKhHy4UdSwXwvnCrEMCz1lqserSNSb61LNnRHlXfQmYPW_OyEHCQ&__tn__=-R&mc_cid=e6dc97c127&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) highlighting the top five reasons to check out this big event!
Governor Holcomb's Week That Was
https://holcombforindiana.com/events-713/?mc_cid=e6dc97c127&mc_eid=[UNIQID]

Indiana: The Fiscal Envy of the Nation!

Great news: Indiana will remain the fiscal envy of the nation! Indiana is closing fiscal year 2019 without needing to dive into the state’s reserves. Indiana’s reserves (kind of like a rainy day savings account) remain at 13.9% of current year expenditures.

To keep Indiana on a roll, Governor Holcomb is recommending that Indiana uses $300 million of reserves to pay for several one-time capital projects and to finish the free-flow of U.S. Highway 31. By paying cash for these projects that will impact Indiana for generations, we'll maintain Indiana's low level of debt, avoid lease obligations over the next 25 years and save taxpayers more than $100 million in interest payments.

And with these savings, we'll have more state funds available in the next budget to tackle our biggest priorities, including putting more money in teacher paychecks to keep Indiana competitive with our Midwestern neighbors.

There's no doubt that it's an exciting time to be a Hoosier, and that's in part to Governor Holcomb's fantastic leadership.

And, of course, Governor Holcomb's week will include a major announcement tomorrow (https://holcombforindiana.com/events-713/?mc_cid=e6dc97c127&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) -- will you be there?
https://holcombforindiana.com/events-713/?mc_cid=e6dc97c127&mc_eid=[UNIQID]

Tomorrow: What You Need to Know

Tomorrow, Governor Holcomb is making a major announcement (https://holcombforindiana.com/events-713/?mc_cid=e6dc97c127&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) at the Hoosier Gym in Knightstown. Planning to be there? Here's what you need to know:

WHEN:
Saturday, July 13th.
Doors open at 11 a.m., event kicks off at 1 p.m. sharp
Please plan to arrive early!

WHERE:
Hoosier Gym
355 N Washington St., Knightstown

PARKING:
Across the street from the Hoosier Gym near the corner of Locust Lane and Adams Street. Please see this linked map (https://gallery.mailchimp.com/89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27/images/2379e83e-0280-4802-832b-9b9082ee8cbb.png?mc_cid=e6dc97c127&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) , and follow the directions of parking attendants on-site. Street parking is also available around the gym when the lot is at capacity.

SOCIAL MEDIA:
Use the hashtag #HolcombCrew, and check out an exclusive Snapchat filter when you arrive!

NOTE:
We've got the party ready for you, so please no homemade signs. Light refreshments, including hot dogs, popcorn, and water, will be available.

CNBC: Indiana is #1 for Infrastructure!

Great news came out this week for Hoosiers! Indiana was named #1 for infrastructure by CNBC! Governor Holcomb has made sure we are living up to our nickname “The Crossroads of America” by making Indiana home to the nation’s only fully funded infrastructure plan. That means investing $32 billion into Indiana infrastructure over the next 20 years. Check out the full story here (https://www.cnbc.com/2019/07/10/5-states-with-the-best-infrastructure-in-america-in-2019.html?mc_cid=e6dc97c127&mc_eid=%5bUNIQID%5d&mc_cid=e6dc97c127&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) !

Get President Trump on the Ballot!

Hoosiers are fired-up to re-elect President Trump and Vice President Pence, but first, we’ve got to get him on the ballot in Indiana – and that requires ballot petition signatures in each of Indiana’s congressional districts.

Would you commit to help collect petition signatures among your family, friends and neighbors? It’s a very easy way to stand with President Donald Trump right now, and help him continue to make America great! Just fill out this form (https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSf2JItBHI6v3IIKv9yky6sxbXMkPhrgArt0fgP8QrDOUW1Gfw/viewform?vc=0&c=0&w=1&fbclid=IwAR18KM_ZWmaNtWoQT1Evs6YIr2qEKSRF73swoC9dwa7-jUEQ5RoZpn_RLgU&mc_cid=e6dc97c127&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) , and we’ll be in touch soon to get you started!

Upcoming Republican Events

subject: Together
disclaimer_text: Paid for by The Markey Committee
body:
| |

| Team,

I wanted to thank you for your tremendous support of my campaign.

I'm grateful to have your on my team in the fight to hold the Trump administration accountable and keep Massachusetts blue in 2020.

Remember that big FEC fundraising deadline we had at the end of June?

**Because of your support, my campaign raised $1 million in Q2!**

**And we hit a benchmark of 9,000 individual contributions from Massachusetts alone.**

Together, we're building a revolution for a Green New Deal that is putting fear in the hearts of the fossil fuel industry across the country.

Together, we're fighting back against President Trump's cruel agenda, Senate Majority Leader Mitch McConnell's obstruction, and Fox News' propaganda machine to progress our Democratic values.

And together, we will win in 2020.

Thank you again for being on my team.

Ed Markey

name: Kamala Harris
subject: Why we fight
body:
A lot is happening in our country right now.

Our so-called leaders are using new fuel to drum up age-old forms of hate
and sow division among us. From ripping babies away from their parents and
imprisoning families seeking asylum in horrible conditions, to referring
to white supremacists as “very fine people” -- and that’s just from the
President.

It’s easy to feel deterred and defeated by it all. I wanted to remind all
of us to never shy away from doing what’s right and why we fight.

It’s our mission to restore our democracy and begin to heal -- and it
starts with all of us. We all have to fight for our America. An America
where we lift each other up instead of putting people down.

I’m calling on you right now because I need your support. Can you pitch in
a contribution to our campaign and
help fight for the best of who we are?

If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your
donation will go through immediately:

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kdh-ema-190712-fr?refcode=ema190712-full&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Englewood&state=FL&zip=34223&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amount=5&express_lane=true&t=1001&refcode2=5571_5755789_nGhYVM&akid=5571%2E5755789%2EnGhYVM ]Chip in $5 now

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kdh-ema-190712-fr?refcode=ema190712-full&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Englewood&state=FL&zip=34223&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amount=250&express_lane=true&t=1005&refcode2=5571_5755789_nGhYVM&akid=5571%2E5755789%2EnGhYVM ]Chip in $250 now

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kdh-ema-190712-fr?refcode=ema190712-full&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Englewood&state=FL&zip=34223&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&t=1006&refcode2=5571_5755789_nGhYVM&akid=5571%2E5755789%2EnGhYVM ]Another Amount

From day one, our campaign has always been what we’re fighting for, not
against.

Together, we’re fighting for health care for all because no one should
ever be forced to choose between putting food on the table and critical,
life-saving medication and care.

Together, we’re fighting for an economy that works for working families.

Together, we’re fighting for safer communities, clean water and air,
better education, immigration reform, reproductive freedom, and every
child to have a shot at a happy, healthy life.

We’re fighting for our values and promises of truth, justice, decency,
equality, and freedom.

That’s the America that I know we can build together. But I need to know
that you’re willing to fight for it -- that you’ll be by my side every
step of the way.

With you, I know we’re unstoppable. If you’re able, can you make
a contribution to our historic
campaign for president? It’s going to take all of us to reclaim our
America:

If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kdh-ema-190712-fr?refcode=ema190712-full&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Englewood&state=FL&zip=34223&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&t=1012&refcode2=5571_5755789_nGhYVM&akid=5571%2E5755789%2EnGhYVM ]Another Amount

Our road ahead will not be easy. But nothing worth fighting for ever is.
Thanks for being with us.

For The People,

— Kamala Harris

body:

| Friend, The Democratic primary got a little more crowded this week when Tom Steyer entered the race. **He 's already pledged to spend at least $100 million of his personal fortune on his bid for president. That puts campaigns like ours at a big disadvantage. ** Look, a large, diverse field of candidates is good for our party. It's so important that this race gets decided by who has the best ideas to lead our party and move our country forward, not by who can spend the most out of their own pocket. I've refused to take money from corporate PACs, oil and pharma executives, and federal lobbyists. I don't want them thinking they own any piece of this. No, this effort is 100% people-powered. I made the decision to build this campaign the right way -- one donation at a time. name: Dick Durbin
subject: Add your name: Pass the Stop Cruelty to Migrant Children Act
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Dick Durbin
body:
#
Peter,
When I visited the border in April, I saw firsthand the cruel and abhorrent conditions. It's enough to make your stomach turn. As Americans, we must demand better of our country than this.
I'm doing everything I can to draw attention to President Trump's self-created humanitarian crisis and ensure that children and migrant families are treated with humanity and compassion. Yesterday, I stood with Senate Democratic leaders and helped introduce the Stop Cruelty to Migrant Children Act to end family separation and the unacceptable conditions at our southern border.
**[Are you with us as we fight to end President Trump's cruel policies? Add your name now. ](http://action.dickdurbin.com/page/m/45ff4c8e/42ab3cf4/6240e6a0/135d74d5/1119294312/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjM0MjIzIiwiSlNWR1NWSlRWRTVCVFVVbEpRPT0iOiJQZXRlciJ9/ "https://durbin.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/pass-the-stop-cruelty-to-migrant-children-act?source=em190712&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&firstname=Peter")**
Democrats and Republicans may not agree on every immigration policy, but safe and humane treatment of children and families should clearly bridge the partisan divide. When children are facing cruelty at the border, it's on all of us to speak out and take action to make it right.
The Stop Cruelty to Migrant Children Act will fight to reverse the family separation policy and ensure that we are no longer inflicting trauma and harm on migrant children and families.
**[As we take on President Trump's anti-immigrant agenda, we need you in this fight with us. Sign on now to call on the Senate to pass the Stop Cruelty to Migrant Children Act.](http://action.dickdurbin.com/page/m/45ff4c8e/42ab3cf4/6240e6a0/135d74d5/1119294312/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjM0MjIzIiwiSlNWR1NWSlRWRTVCVFVVbEpRPT0iOiJQZXRlciJ9/ "https://durbin.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/pass-the-stop-cruelty-to-migrant-children-act?source=em190712&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&firstname=Peter")**
My best,
Dick Durbin
**[Sign the Petition](http://action.dickdurbin.com/page/m/45ff4c8e/42ab3cf4/6240e6a0/135d74d5/1119294312/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjM0MjIzIiwiSlNWR1NWSlRWRTVCVFVVbEpRPT0iOiJQZXRlciJ9/ "https://durbin.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/pass-the-stop-cruelty-to-migrant-children-act?source=em190712&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&firstname=Peter")**
